t_ch: 行至此处者，尚可归去吗？归于神之怀抱者，理当融于那永恒的幸福之中……
t_en: Can those who have come this far still turn back? Those who return to the embrace of the gods should merge into that eternal joy...

t_ch: 自那深深处，流动的异彩卷作涡旋；那是泥泞的星云、剧毒的花蜜、腥甜的融泥，是某个庞然之物沉眠萎败的一角——
t_en: From the depths below, swirling colors twist into a vortex. A murky nebula, venomous nectar, sickeningly sweet sludge... a glimpse of something colossal lying dormant and decay...

t_ch: 你的感官难以抑制地为之所吸引，灵魂亦然；就像污水浸透棉帛，你感受到无孔不入的泥泞，正势不可挡地向着「你」这个存在攀爬而来，呼唤着你——
t_en: Your senses are drawn to it irresistibly, as is your soul. Like sewage soaking through cotton, you feel the all-pervasive mud crawling toward the very essence of "you," unstoppable, beckoning...

t_ch: 前来这里，与「我」融为一体，回归宇宙万物起初的模样——始亦是终，一即是万、万亦是一，这里万物永恒流溢、永恒静寂……
t_en: Come here, become one with "me," and return to the primordial state of all things in the universe, where the beginning is also the end, the one is the all, and the all is the one. Here, everything flows eternally in everlasting silence...

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 机铠对战海报
t_en: Mechatron Battle Poster

t_ch: 新一届机铠对战大赛即将开幕，现正火热报名中。
t_en: The next Mechatron Battle Tournament is almost here! Sign up now to join the action.

t_ch: 武道宗师，格斗之王，用你的实力赢取百万奖金，改变人生吧！
t_en: Martial arts masters, kings of fighters... Use your skills to win a million-credit prize and change your life!

t_ch: 由于观众对机器人大战的热情，机铠对战一度成为了演艺圈的跳板，数十位著名的机铠武打演员都曾在对战赛中取得耀眼的成绩。
t_en: With everyone so hyped for robot fights, mechatron combat has become a massive stepping stone for showbiz. Tons of famous mechatron action stars actually started their careers in these matches.

t_ch: 机铠模型（简装型）
t_en: Mechatron Model (Standard Edition)

t_ch: 大批量生产的低规格机铠玩具，因其简单的拼装方式与亲民的价格饱受欢迎。
t_en: These mass-produced, entry-level mechatron toys are a huge hit thanks to how easy they are to build and their affordable prices.

t_ch: 有时候，量产机的人气，或许会超乎你的想象。
t_en: You'd be surprised how much people love these mass-produced models!

t_ch: 便携改造件
t_en: Portable Modification Parts

t_ch: 从头到脚，为每位机铠提供最精致的改造选择，现在购买，还增异色替换件一盒。
t_en: Get the most stylish mods for your mechatron from head to toe. Buy now and we'll even throw in a set of alternate color parts!

t_ch: 零件请认准珠星财团，质量有口碑，售后有保障。
t_en: Stick with Pearluxe Corp for all your parts. Their quality is top-notch, and the after-sales support is second to none.

t_ch: 喷漆
t_en: Spray Paint

t_ch: 珠星牌喷漆，限定变色龙款，五彩斑斓的黑与五光十色的白，和我们一起，成为最靓的机铠。
t_en: Pearluxe Spray Paint, Limited Chameleon Edition! Featuring "iridescent black" and "prismatic white." Join the club and be the trendiest mechatron around.

t_ch: 珠星牌喷漆，无毒无味无污染，一秒上色，三秒固着，使用前记得摇一摇哦。
t_en: Pearluxe Spray Paint: non-toxic, odorless, and eco-friendly! Applies in a second and sets in three. Just remember to give it a good shake before use!

t_ch: （先问问列车长发生了什么事吧……）
t_en: (Ask the conductor about what happened...)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你回来啦！
t_en: Oh good, you're here, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 怪东西在哪呢？
t_en: Where is the strange thing you were talking about?

t_ch: 列车长有难，岂能坐视不管。
t_en: The conductor is in trouble, we can't just stand around and do nothing.

t_ch: 不知道，但就在列车里帕！没有人比帕姆更熟悉阿基维利的列车了，车里有奇怪的东西，帕姆就是能感觉到！
t_en: Pom-Pom doesn't know, but it's in the Express! No one knows Akivili's Express better than Pom-Pom. There is something strange in here, Pom-Pom can feel it!

t_ch: 你也一起来找吧，一定要找出来。
t_en: Help Pom-Pom look for it. We have to find it.

t_ch: 一定要找出来帕！
t_en: We have to find it!

t_ch: 你也被叫回来了？看来帕姆这次是认真的……
t_en: Did they send for you as well? Looks like Pom-Pom is quite serious this time...

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生有察觉到异样吗？
t_en: Mr. Yang, did you notice anything unusual?

t_ch: 没有。我对危险和敌意还算敏锐，但列车里没有这种感觉。
t_en: No. I have a heightened sense of hostility and danger, but I don't sense anything on the Express.

t_ch: 银河这么大，或许有某种能够隐形的生物来到了我们的列车……
t_en: The galaxy is huge. Maybe there's some kind of organism that can become invisible visiting us...

t_ch: 列车长说的怪东西是什么啊？
t_en: What is this strange thing the conductor is referring to?

t_ch: 不清楚，帕姆也说不上来，它只是有种直觉。
t_en: Not sure, Pom-Pom couldn't put it into words. It's like a gut feeling.

t_ch: 但关于列车的事，我倾向于相信帕姆的直觉。
t_en: I lean towards trusting Pom-Pom's instinct when it has to do with the Express.

t_ch: 三月七和丹恒呢？
t_en: Where are March 7th and Dan Heng?

t_ch: 姬子让他们去别的车厢了，这里有我们就足够啦。
t_en: Himeko had them go to a different coach. We can take care of what's going on here.

t_ch: 好吧…
t_en: Okay...

t_ch: 呀，你也回来啦…辛苦你了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Oh hey {NICKNAME}, thanks for coming!

t_ch: 姬子有察觉到异样吗？
t_en: Himeko, did you notice anything unusual?

t_ch: 没有呢，但既然帕姆这么担心，就一定有什么东西在列车里吧。
t_en: Nope, but there must be something on the Express if Pom-Pom is getting so worked up.

t_ch: 不要紧的，如果大家找不到，我还有别的办法…虽说花费在这种事上有点遗憾，但总不能让列车长一直烦恼下去啊。
t_en: Don't worry, I have another way to deal with this if no one can find anything... It's just a little wasteful to use it on something like this. But we can't let it continue to stress the conductor either.

t_ch: 列车长好像很焦虑…
t_en: The conductor seems very anxious...

t_ch: 嗯，它对星穹列车有很深的感情，会危害这辆列车的事情总是让列车长特别焦虑呢。
t_en: Yep, Pom-Pom has a deep connection with the Astral Express. Anything that could harm the Express always stresses out the conductor.

t_ch: …所以你们也帮忙教育教育阿七，让她不要随便鼓捣列车上的设备喔。
t_en: So... help me educate March about not messing with the equipment on the Express.

t_ch: 我能做些什么呢？
t_en: What can I do?

t_ch: 检查一下车厢，看看有没有什么奇怪的地方吧。
t_en: Check the coach and see if there is anything out of the ordinary.

t_ch: 我去帮忙了。
t_en: Will do.

t_ch: 咖啡机似乎没什么异样。你试着做了杯咖啡，味道不错，至少比姬子请你喝的要好……
t_en: There doesn't seem to be anything wrong with the coffee machine. You make a cup of coffee, and it tastes good, at least better than Himeko's.

t_ch: 你仔仔细细检查了每一片叶子，每一条根茎，甚至翻开了每一块泥土，并没有发现躲藏着的神秘间谍。
t_en: You scrutinize every leaf and rhizome, turn over every piece of dirt, and do not find anything suspicious.

t_ch: 灯泡散发着柔和的光线，你狠狠地瞪着它。
t_en: The light bulb emits a soft glow, and you stare hard at it.

t_ch: 一面镜子吸引了你的注意…你感到疑惑——
t_en: A mirror catches your eye... You're confused.

t_ch: 哪来的镜子？
t_en: Where's the mirror from?

t_ch: 确实。你不记得列车上曾经有这么一面镜子。
t_en: You don't remember there being such a mirror on the Express.

t_ch: 破了也不修修？
t_en: The mirror is broken. Why isn't it repaired?

t_ch: 镜子对你的善意表示感谢，但问题是，它是什么时候出现的？你不记得列车上曾经有这么一面镜子。
t_en: The mirror is grateful for your kindness. But the question is... when did it appear? You don't remember there being such a mirror on the Express.

t_ch: 我咋这么好看？
t_en: Why am I so pretty?

t_ch: 这是个非常棒的问题，可惜镜子无法回答你，你甚至不记得列车上曾经有这么一面镜子。
t_en: That's a great question, but the mirror can't answer it. You don't even remember there being such a mirror on the Express.

t_ch: 更奇怪的是：列车上的其他人似乎都对这突然出现的镜子视若无睹…也许你应该再检查下它，或者问问别人有什么看法。要节约时间的话，就选择前者。
t_en: What's even stranger is that everyone else on the train seems to turn a blind eye to the sudden appearance of the mirror... Maybe you should re-check the mirror or ask the others about it. If you want to save time, you should check it yourself.

t_ch: 你也被叫回来了？看来帕姆这次是认真的……
t_en: Did they send for you as well? Looks like Pom-Pom is quite serious this time...

t_ch: 那面镜子是怎么回事？
t_en: What's the deal with that mirror?

t_ch: 镜子…？
t_en: Mirror...?

t_ch: 就在那儿啊。
t_en: The one right there.

t_ch: （瓦尔特先生好像真的看不见它……）
t_en: (Looks like Mr. Yang really can't see it...)

t_ch: 看不见的镜子？我好像听说过……
t_en: An invisible mirror? I think I've heard something about that...

t_ch: 呀，你也回来啦…辛苦你了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Oh hey {NICKNAME}, thanks for coming!

t_ch: 那面镜子是怎么回事？
t_en: What's the deal with that mirror?

t_ch: 你说什么？镜子？
t_en: What are you talking about? What mirror?

t_ch: 就在那儿啊。
t_en: The one right there.

t_ch: …没看见什么镜子啊。
t_en: I don't see a mirror...

t_ch: （好像只有我能看见它…）
t_en: (Seems like I'm the only one who can see it...)

t_ch: 那里什么也没有呀。
t_en: There's nothing there.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客发现什么了吗！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, have you found something?

t_ch: 有一面奇怪的镜子…
t_en: There's this strange mirror...

t_ch: 呜哇！帕帕帕姆就知道一定定定有什么古怪！
t_en: Wahh! P—P—Pom-Pom knew something strange was going on!

t_ch: 什么也没有…
t_en: There's nothing.

t_ch: 呜哇！连你也没有发现……
t_en: Wahh! Even you didn't find anything...?

t_ch: 赶快想想办法，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Come on, {NICKNAME}, think of something!

t_ch: 赶快想想办法，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Come on, {NICKNAME}, think of something!

t_ch: 你靠近镜子，看见镜中一道身影慢慢显现——却一点也不像你。镜子里是一个戴着兜帽的女孩，脸部被古怪的面罩遮挡，看不清她的样子。
t_en: You approach the mirror and see a figure slowly emerging in it — but it doesn't look like you at all. In the mirror is a girl in a hood, her face hidden by a strange mask, making it impossible to make out her appearance.

t_ch: 还没等你反应过来，女孩就跨出了镜子。
t_en: Before you can react, the girl steps out of the mirror.

t_ch: 哇喔，这就是「星穹列车」吗？
t_en: Wow, so this is the Astral Express?

t_ch: 是的，没错…
t_en: Yes, that's right.

t_ch: 那太好了——
t_en: Huh, perfect.

t_ch: 你是谁啊——
t_en: Who are you...

t_ch: 噢，还没有自我介绍……
t_en: Oh, I haven't introduced myself yet...

t_ch: 妖怪，吃我一棒！
t_en: Take this, you monster!

t_ch: 话音未落，你就抡起棒球棍砸向女孩。球棒穿过她的身体，又穿过镜子，仿佛她们根本不存在。
t_en: Before any words are spoken, you swing your baseball bat at the girl. The bat goes through her body and through the mirror as if they don't even exist.

t_ch: 别动手呀！我不是坏人——
t_en: Wait a minute! I'm not one of the bad guys!

t_ch: 我是「流光忆庭」的信使，接下来的日子要借宿贵列车，请多指教！
t_en: I'm a messenger from the Garden of Recollection. I will be staying on the Express from now on. It's very nice to meet you!

t_ch: 呃，请多指教…
t_en: Err, it's nice to meet you too.

t_ch: 谢谢！你真友善～不过，还是让我解释一下吧——
t_en: Thank you! You're a friendly one! However, I should still explain—

t_ch: 经过车主同意了吗？
t_en: Do you have the approval of the train's owner?

t_ch: 呃，所以我主动来见你了呀，让我解释一下——
t_en: Erm, that's why I'm here. Let me explain—

t_ch: 流光忆庭？啥玩意。
t_en: Garden of Recollection? What is that?

t_ch: 让我解释一下——
t_en: Let me explain—

t_ch: 「流光忆庭」是追随星神浮黎的组织，作为「记忆」的信使，我们放弃了肉身，以模因的形式穿梭于宇宙，为浮黎搜寻星海间最珍贵的记忆。
t_en: Garden of Recollection is an organization that worships the Aeon Fuli. As a messenger for the Remembrance, we surrendered our worldly bodies and traverse the universe in the form of memes to search the stars on behalf of Fuli for the most precious memories.

t_ch: 我能够来到这里，就说明你一定认知过「记忆」和「流光忆庭」的概念。你所持有的记忆为忆庭开启了复制的信道，将我植入到你的脑海里……
t_en: Seeing that I'm able to be here, you must have already come to understand the concept of Remembrance and the Garden of Recollection. The memories that you possess have activated a pathway for the Garden of Recollection to make copies and implant me into your thoughts...

t_ch: 其实我们可以悄悄地跟随你，没有人会发现。不过，忆庭认为你是特别的：你拥有的是极其稀有的宝贵记忆，并且未来还会得到更多……
t_en: We can actually just follow you without being noticed. However, the Garden of Recollection believes that you're special: The memories that you possess are extremely rare and precious, and it's very likely there'll be even more in the future...

t_ch: 所以我想还是和你打声招呼比较好！我注意到你还接受过「窃忆者」的概念——忆庭可不希望你把我们当成小偷或窃贼！我们所做的一切，都是为了崇高的目的。
t_en: That's why I thought it'd be best if I introduced myself to you! I noticed that you've also received the concept of Memosnatchers... The Garden of Recollection wouldn't want you to mistake us for thieves or crooks! Everything we do is for a higher purpose.

t_ch: 现在，我们就算认识啦？
t_en: I believe we've become acquainted now.

t_ch: 我什么时候认知过那些概念…
t_en: When did I come across those things before...

t_ch: 关键词是「黑塔」和「光锥」——你在与上述概念接触时，植入了「记忆」、「流光忆庭」与「窃忆者」。
t_en: Some keywords and hints to your question: Herta and Light Cone — when you came in contact with them, the concept of Remembrance, Garden of Recollection, and Memosnatcher were implanted in you.

t_ch: 黑塔女士对我们有点成见。因此忆庭认为，还是由我给予你正确的概念比较好。
t_en: Madam Herta has a slight prejudice against us, so the Garden of Recollection believes that it'll be best if I provide you with a proper understanding of these concepts.

t_ch: 你们有什么崇高的目的？
t_en: What is your noble cause?

t_ch: 我们要让宇宙不会因「毁灭」而遭到忘却。
t_en: We want to prevent the universe from being forgotten because of the Destruction.

t_ch: 即使物质泯灭了，只要它还存在于记忆中，就将一直活下去。
t_en: Even if all matter is annihilated, it can continue to live on in the form of memories.

t_ch: 你这样上车跟小偷有啥区别？
t_en: So you're just smuggling yourself on board the express?

t_ch: 那当然不一样啦，我没有肉体，因此算不上偷渡。
t_en: There's a big difference, of course. I don't have a material body, so it doesn't count as smuggling.

t_ch: 所以你是…二次元人？
t_en: So, you're a... 2D character?

t_ch: 嗯，就把我当成招贴画上的人好了。你总不能指责海报里的卡通角色偷渡吧？
t_en: Yup, just imagine me as someone from the posters. It would be strange if you were to scold someone from the cartoons for smuggling, right?

t_ch: 所以你是…肉体窃贼？
t_en: So, you're a... burglar of physical bodies?

t_ch: 我都自愿放弃了，还偷它干嘛！
t_en: Why would I want to steal a material body? I gave up my own willingly!

t_ch: 和你说这些，我也不是毫无所求～别怪我说话直白哦，{NICKNAME}，忆庭想要保存你的记忆。
t_en: There is actually something that I need. So I'll get right to it. {NICKNAME}, the Garden of Recollection wants to preserve your memories.

t_ch: 但是别担心，我们不会「夺走」它，只想从你那里复制珍贵的片段。你可以把它看作一笔交易……
t_en: But don't worry, we won't take it from you. We just want to make a copy of the precious moments. You can see it as a trade between us...

t_ch: 这是专为保存你的记忆而创造的「忘却之庭」。我们希望你能够定期来到这里，重新唤起失落的回忆。忆庭将从中取走想要的，然后给予你回报——以物质和光锥的形式。
t_en: This is the Forgotten Hall created specially to store your memories. We hope that you can visit this place regularly to rekindle your lost memories. The Garden of Recollection will extract the memories they want and give you something in return — in the form of matter and Light Cones.

t_ch: 来吧，我以旧身的记忆发誓：你不会有任何损失。
t_en: Come, I swear on the memories of my past self that you won't be at a loss for anything.

t_ch: 怎么啦，不舒服吗？
t_en: What is it? Are you not feeling well?

t_ch: 还不大适应…
t_en: I don't think I'm used to this...

t_ch: 第一次都会这样的啦。
t_en: That's how it is the first time.

t_ch: 没事，我好得很。
t_en: It's nothing, I feel fine.

t_ch: 啊，那就好，我还怕你生气了呢。
t_en: Ah, that's good. I was afraid you'd be angry.

t_ch: 怎么里面还要战斗啊。
t_en: Why do we still have to fight inside?

t_ch: 强烈的刺激会让你放松对潜意识的防守，只有这样，我们才能复制你的记忆呀。
t_en: A strong stimulus will lower your guard against your subconscious. Only then will we be able to copy your memories.

t_ch: 以后，你只需要定期进入「忘却之庭」就可以了。我们会给予你丰富的回报。
t_en: From now on, all you have to do is enter the Forgotten Hall periodically, and we'll give you an abundant amount of rewards.

t_ch: 呃，对了——我停留在星穹列车上的事情，最好只有你和我知道。我不会在你的伙伴们面前现身，所以你也不要说，好吗？
t_en: Erm, about me being on the Astral Express... It'd be best if you kept it between the two of us. I won't reveal myself to your companions, so make sure you don't tell them about me, okay?

t_ch: 不行，我有话直说。
t_en: No, I have to tell them.

t_ch: 那样的话，忆庭只好屏蔽他们的记忆了…别逼我这么做呀，我也是有苦衷的！
t_en: If that's the case, the Garden of Recollection will have to block out their memories... I really don't have a choice, please don't make me do that!

t_ch: 这是你和我的秘密。
t_en: This is our little secret.

t_ch: 太好了，谢谢你{NICKNAME}，很高兴你是个通情达理的人。
t_en: Perfect! Thank you, {NICKNAME}. I'm glad you're so pleasant to work with.

t_ch: 那我怎么交差呢？
t_en: Then what should I tell everyone?

t_ch: 呃，你可以说是一株相位灵火，或者异问魔。它们出现消失都很常见，应该能糊弄过去……
t_en: Erm, you can say that it's just a Phase Flame or an Inquisition Spirit. They often suddenly appear and then vanish. You should be able to get past them with that...

t_ch: 好啦，想再见到我，你就靠近镜子吧。
t_en: Alrighty then, just step close to the mirror if you ever want to find me.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你找到什么了吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, did you find anything?

t_ch: （说出实情。）
t_en: (Tell the truth.)

t_ch: 「流光忆庭」的人在这里？在列车上……
t_en: Someone from the Garden of Recollection is here? On the Express...?

t_ch: 看来传闻是真的，「记忆」的令使们没有实体，只允许想被看见的人找到他们。
t_en: Seems like the rumors are true. The Emanators of the Remembrance don't have a physical body and only those they allow can see them.

t_ch: 列车才不是随随便便让他们待着玩的地方帕！一定要把她赶走！
t_en: The Express is not a place they can just come and go as they wish! We have to kick them out!

t_ch: 嗯，我也这么认为，和忆庭打交道太危险了。关于「记忆」的交易也…十分蹊跷。
t_en: Yes, I agree. Dealing with the Garden of Recollection is dangerous, and the trade of memories is... a shady business.

t_ch: 我们去研究对策，列车长，放心吧。
t_en: We'll come up with something. Don't worry, conductor.

t_ch: 嗯，拜托了帕！
t_en: Alright, thanks!

t_ch: （替信使隐瞒。）
t_en: (Cover up for the Messenger.)

t_ch: 异问魔…？在列车上？
t_en: An Inquisition Spirit...? On the Express?

t_ch: 嗯，这种生物确实会突然出现，向遇到的第一个人提问…{NICKNAME}，它问了你什么？
t_en: Yeah, these creatures do suddenly pop up sometimes and start questioning the first person they see... {NICKNAME}, what did it ask you?

t_ch: 它问…1+1为什么=2？
t_en: It asked... Why does one plus one equal two?

t_ch: 这倒证明了我一直以来的猜想：看来异问魔应该是「智识」的造物。
t_en: This actually confirms a theory that I've always had: It seems like Inquisition Spirits are a creation of the Erudition.

t_ch: 它问…它应该问什么？
t_en: It asked... What it should ask me?

t_ch: 这倒证明了我一直以来的猜想：看来异问魔应该是「神秘」的造物。
t_en: This actually confirms a theory that I've always had: It seems like Inquisition Spirits are a creation of the Enigmata.

t_ch: 它问…最近有什么高兴的事？
t_en: It asked... if anything joyous happened recently.

t_ch: 这倒证明了我一直以来的猜想：看来异问魔应该是「欢愉」的造物。
t_en: This actually confirms a theory that I've always had: It seems like Inquisition Spirits are a creation of the Elation.

t_ch: 谁的造物都不行帕！一定要把它赶走！
t_en: Pom-Pom doesn't care whose creation it is! It has to go!

t_ch: 是的，瓦尔特，你知道怎么做吗？
t_en: Indeed. Welt, do you know what to do?

t_ch: 别担心，它应该已经走了——异问魔一次只问一个问题，得到答案后，它就会去另一个地方。
t_en: Don't worry, it should be gone by now — Inquisition Spirits only ask one question at a time. It will go elsewhere once it gets an answer.

t_ch: 那就好……
t_en: That's good...

t_ch: （就这样吧……）
t_en: (That should do it...)

t_ch: （咦，镜子还在……）
t_en: (Hmm? The mirror is still here...)
Hmph...

t_ch: 我都那样拜托你了，你还是告诉他们了啊。
t_en: I asked you so candidly yet you still told them.

t_ch: 我信赖他们。
t_en: I have trust in them.

t_ch: 我不相信你。
t_en: I don't trust you.

t_ch: 对不起…
t_en: Sorry...

t_ch: 哼，那我就只好封锁了他们的这段记忆——抱歉，但我没得选。
t_en: Hmph, then I'll have to seal this portion of their memories — I'm sorry, but I don't have a choice.

t_ch: 我的事情除了你以外，谁也不能知道。一旦发现他们记忆里出现了我的关键词，就只能修改掉了…你为什么不相信我？我不会伤害他们的。
t_en: You and only you can know about me. If a keyword related to me shows up in their memories, then we'll have no choice but to get rid of it... Why don't you trust me? I won't hurt them.

t_ch: 但是忆庭…我们有自己的法则。
t_en: *sigh* We have our rules at the Garden of Recollection.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。很抱歉让你说了谎，请理解，流光忆庭…我们有自己的法则。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. I'm sorry you had to lie, but please do understand... We have our own rules at the Garden of Recollection.

t_ch: 你要进入「忘却之庭」吗？
t_en: Do you want to enter the Forgotten Hall?

t_ch: 进入。
t_en: Enter.

t_ch: 我想问你点事…
t_en: I wish to ask you...

t_ch: 好呀，你问吧。
t_en: Sure, ask away.

t_ch: 关于浮黎和流光忆庭…
t_en: About Fuli and the Garden of Recollection...

t_ch: 我尽量解答～
t_en: I'll try to answer to the best of my abilities!

t_ch: 说说「记忆」浮黎吧？
t_en: Tell me about Fuli the Remembrance.

t_ch: 我可以给你关于浮黎大人的记忆……
t_en: I can give you the memories related to the Aeon, Fuli...

t_ch: 少女挥了挥手，仿佛掷出一枚小石子，水面不断荡开涟漪。你突然想起——
t_en: The girl waved her hand, as if tossing a small pebble that caused the water surface to keep rippling. You suddenly remember—

t_ch: 浮黎，行于「记忆」命途的星神。祂为行将毁灭的宇宙保留下珍贵的回忆，以待他日重生之时所需…记忆就在这里戛然而止。
t_en: Fuli, the Aeon on the Path of Remembrance, preserves the precious memories of a dying universe for its eventual rebirth... Here, the memories abruptly come to an end.

t_ch: 只有这些啦。我可没有荣幸保留亲睹那位大人的体验。
t_en: That's all I have. I don't have the honor of keeping the experience of seeing the Aeon, Fuli with my own eyes.

t_ch: 介绍下流光忆庭？
t_en: Tell me about the Garden of Recollection.

t_ch: 哎？我记得我向你解释过的吧？你还真健忘耶。
t_en: Huh? I thought I already told you about that. You really are forgetful.

t_ch: 你突然想起少女曾说过的话，言犹在耳——
t_en: You suddenly remember what the girl had said, and the words begin ringing in your ears—

t_ch: 「流光忆庭」是追随星神浮黎的组织，作为「记忆」的信使，我们放弃了肉身，以模因的形式穿梭于宇宙，为浮黎搜寻星海间最珍贵的记忆。
t_en: Garden of Recollection is an organization that worships the Aeon Fuli. As a messenger for the Remembrance, we surrendered our worldly bodies and traverse the universe in the form of memes to search the stars on behalf of Fuli for the most precious memories.

t_ch: 想起来了吗？
t_en: Do you remember now?

t_ch: 收集记忆用来做什么？
t_en: What is the purpose of collecting memories?

t_ch: 「有形之物的消逝，必然带来无形之物的湮灭。」
t_en: "The disappearance of those with form will inevitably bring about the obliteration of those without."

t_ch: 你瞧这片宇宙，反物质军团的风暴扫过，文明的星火就像风中残烛。即便没有纳努克，许多世界也摇摇欲坠，步入黄昏。任由文明的记忆消逝是不可饶恕的破灭。
t_en: Look at the galaxy. After the incident with the Antimatter Legion, civilizations are like dying embers in the wind. Many worlds wither and crumble even without Nanook's influence. It is unacceptable to allow the memories of civilizations to simply vanish.

t_ch: 所以忆庭决定收集并保管他们的记忆。灾难结束后，这些记忆的碎片将为消逝的一切拼出重生的蓝图。
t_en: That is why Garden of Recollection has decided to collect and safe keep their memories. Once the crisis ends, the fragments of these memories will shape a blueprint of rebirth for everything that has disappeared.

t_ch: 我想问别的…
t_en: I'd like to ask about something else...

t_ch: 关于你…
t_en: About you...

t_ch: 我？
t_en: Me?

t_ch: 失去肉身是什么感觉？
t_en: What does it feel like to lose your physical body?

t_ch: 更轻松，更自由。
t_en: It's more relaxing, and freer.

t_ch: 随着女孩的一字一句，你感到肉体沉重、滞缓、疲惫，它是水泥浇成的外壳，举手投足慢如背壳的蜗牛，慢如一个纪元的缓缓流逝……
t_en: With each of the girl's words, you feel heavier, sluggish, and tired. Your body is a shell caste from concrete and its movements are as slow as a snail, as slow as an era gradually passing by...

t_ch: 现在的我不用再担心极寒和高热、痛痒饥渴、不必担心刀枪和太阳磁暴——我不再是人啦。
t_en: I don't have to worry about the cold, heat, pain, hunger, flesh wounds, or solar storms — I'm not human anymore.

t_ch: 没有肉身，没有肾上腺素、后叶催产素、多巴胺和其他让人喜怒哀乐的东西。这也给了我一种更中立的角度去审视人类的思想和欲求。可以说，我离自由意志更近一步了。
t_en: Without a material body, I don't have adrenaline, oxytocin, dopamine, or anything that causes different emotions in people. This allows me to judge human thought and desire with an even more neutral position. You can say that I'm another step closer to having free will.

t_ch: 当然，这么方便也不是没有代价的——代价是我得凭借人们的记忆才能四处活动。唔…所以这种存在形式究竟算不算自由呢？
t_en: Of course, there is a price for the convenience — the price is that I rely on the memories of others to move around. Erm... And would you consider this kind of existence to be free?

t_ch: 女孩突然陷入了沉思。
t_en: The girl suddenly enters a state of deep contemplation.

t_ch: 你能读取别人的记忆？
t_en: Are you able to read other people's memories?

t_ch: 是啊。我只能借着人们的记忆活动。我的每一次出现，移动，消失，都是在读取某人的记忆啊。
t_en: Yes. I'm only able to move around due to the memories of others. Every time I appear, move, or disappear, it means I'm reading someone's memories.

t_ch: …如果是担心我会偷看、窃取、操作你的记忆，那你就多虑了。忆者接受过严格的训练，任何违背律令的记忆操作会立刻终止我的存续。
t_en: But you don't have to worry about me peeping, stealing, or controlling your memories. Memokeepers have undergone rigorous training. Anything we do that goes against the rules will result in the immediate termination of our existence.

t_ch: 虽说有些「窃忆者」能绕开禁令…但我不是那种人！
t_en: Although some Memosnatchers can bypass the restrictions... Um, I'm not one of them!

t_ch: 你长什么样？
t_en: What do you actually look like?

t_ch: 好奇我面具之下的长相吗？哼…那就拿足够份量的回忆来和我交换吧！
t_en: Are you curious about what I look like under this mask? Hmph... Then bring me enough memories to exchange for it!

t_ch: 我想问别的…
t_en: I'd like to ask about something else...

t_ch: 关于我的记忆…
t_en: About my memories...

t_ch: 嗯，你说吧。
t_en: Mhm, go ahead.

t_ch: 能恢复我失去的记忆吗？
t_en: Are you able to restore my lost memories?

t_ch: 你…在试探我？
t_en: Are you... testing me?

t_ch: 不行，这是被禁止的。我可是守法忆者，我拒绝！
t_en: No, that's forbidden. I'm a rule-abiding Memokeeper. I refuse!

t_ch: 为什么我的记忆很宝贵？
t_en: Why are my memories precious?

t_ch: 你可是{RUBY_B#万界之癌}星核 {RUBY_E#}的载体耶！能在那样一股力量进入身体后还好好活着，甚至使用它的力量……
t_en: You're a receptacle for a {RUBY_B#Cancer of All Worlds}Stellaron{RUBY_E#}! You're able to survive and even wield its power...

t_ch: 这样的人会拥有怎样的过去和未来，又将为宇宙留下怎样的忆质？流光忆庭对此很有兴趣。
t_en: The Garden of Recollection is very interested in what kind of a past, future, and influence on the universe someone like this will have.

t_ch: 为什么只有我能记得你？
t_en: Why am I the only one who remembers you?

t_ch: 很抱歉，我限制了别人对我的回忆。
t_en: I'm sorry, but I've restricted others from having memories of me.

t_ch: 每个人对自我、对外物的记忆都是独一无二的，为了保持它的独立性和纯粹，就让咱们的交易成为仅限于你我之间的小秘密吧？
t_en: Everyone has a unique memory of themselves and everything around them. In order to keep that uniqueness and pureness unspoiled, let's keep our dealings a secret.

t_ch: 我想问别的…
t_en: I'd like to ask about something else...

t_ch: 我没问题了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 不，再见。
t_en: No, goodbye.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 进入「忘却之庭」。
t_en: Enter the Forgotten Hall.

t_ch: 商店「光锥呈现」。
t_en: Shop "Light Cone Manifest"

t_ch: 你看着镜子旁的女孩：该怎么向同伴交代呢？她像是读出了你的心声，轻轻一笑。
t_en: You look at the girl in the mirror. How will you explain this to your companions? She laughs softly, as if she saw through your concerns.

t_ch: 谢谢你，仙舟之行的回忆，已经保存下来了～
t_en: Thank you. I've already kept this journey aboard the Xianzhou in my memories.

t_ch: 演习什么时候开始？
t_en: When are we starting drills?

t_ch: 啊，原来你是来赴这个约的，太好了，我就知道能指望你。
t_en: Ah, so you're keeping this appointment, then. That's great. I knew I could count on you.

t_ch: 我已经把禁区里的年轻铁卫都动员起来了，他们听说能跟你这个传奇人物交手，都兴奋得不得了。
t_en: I've mobilized all the young Silvermane Guards in the restricted zone, and they're all beyond pumped to be fighting against a legend like yourself.

t_ch: 相信我，这次你想穿过铁卫的防线，难度绝对要大上好几倍！
t_en: Trust me, you'll find it several times tougher this time if you want to break through the defensive line!

t_ch: 等你准备好了就跟我的副官聊聊吧——演习随时可以开始，我们只需要把禁区的场地快速布置一下就行。
t_en: Speak with my aide when you're ready — we can start the drill any time. It just takes a moment to set everything up in the restricted zone.

t_ch: 哔——啵。
t_en: Beep — boop.

t_ch: 向你致敬，教官。
t_en: Hats off to you, drillmaster.

t_ch: 你就是邓恩的副官？
t_en: Are you Dunn's aide?

t_ch: 也向你致敬。
t_en: Hats off to you too, my guy.

t_ch: 银鬃铁卫后勤安保部第三支队副队长，「副官」，向您报道。哔——啵。
t_en: Aide, deputy head of division 3 of the Silvermane Guards' logistics and security department, reporting for duty. Beep — boop.

t_ch: 随时可以开始铁卫军事演练。一切听您部署，教官。
t_en: Silvermane drills are ready to go. Everything is waiting on your command, drillmaster.

t_ch: 哔——啵。
t_en: Beep — boop.

t_ch: 向你致敬，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: I salute you, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 下一场演练什么时候开始？
t_en: When's the next drill starting?

t_ch: 只要本「副官」一声令下，随时可以开始。
t_en: As long as I give the order, it can start at any time.

t_ch: 你想立刻开始第二轮禁区演练吗，{NICKNAME}教官？
t_en: Would you like to start round two now, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 开始吧。
t_en: Let's begin.

t_ch: 明白。立即开始布置禁区演练的场地。
t_en: Got it. Setting up the venue for restricted zone drills.

t_ch: 给我介绍下这轮演练的目标。
t_en: Fill me in on my target for this round.

t_ch: 这一次的演练目标是——击败「少校」。
t_en: The goal of this drill is to defeat the Major.

t_ch: 另外，你还需要再次调度禁区里的齿轮栈桥。
t_en: You'll also need to configure the gear bridge in the restricted zone once again.

t_ch: 既要脑子，也要肌肉。哔——啵。
t_en: It takes not just brains but also brawn. Beep-boop.

t_ch: 下次吧。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 明白。一切服从你的安排，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: Got it. Everything's been done as per your order, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你好啊，{F#小妹妹}{M#小哥}，在「画中世界」各处穿行的时候还习惯吗？
t_en: Hey there, {F#young lady.}{M#buddy!} You getting the hang of traversing the World in Canvas yet?

t_ch: 需要的话可以在我这儿练习一番？
t_en: Need some practice? You can always try your luck with me.

t_ch: 试试看。
t_en: Give it a try.

t_ch: 算了。
t_en: Never mind.

t_ch: 想要练习的话，可以随时来找我。
t_en: If you ever want to practice, come find me anytime.

t_ch: 先来点简单的练练手吧。
t_en: Let's start with something easy to warm up.

t_ch: 给我整点有挑战性的！
t_en: Give me something challenging!

t_ch: 居然要挑战高级难度？很难的哦！你确定吗？
t_en: You're going for the hard difficulty? It's really tough! You sure about this?

t_ch: 还用问？
t_en: Do you even need to ask?

t_ch: 你唬住我了，先等等。
t_en: Whoa, hold on a sec. You got me there.

t_ch: 哔——啵。
t_en: Beep-boop.

t_ch: 向你致敬，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: I salute you, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 下一场演练什么时候开始？
t_en: When's the next drill starting?

t_ch: 只要本「副官」一声令下，随时可以开始。
t_en: As long as I give the order, it can start at any time.

t_ch: 你想立刻开始第三轮禁区演练吗，{NICKNAME}教官？
t_en: Would you like to start round three now, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 开始吧。
t_en: Let's begin.

t_ch: 明白。立即开始布置禁区演练的场地。
t_en: Got it. Setting up the venue for restricted zone drills.

t_ch: 给我介绍下这轮演练的规则。
t_en: Fill me in on the rules for this round.

t_ch: 数量取胜。
t_en: Quantity wins.

t_ch: 没错。最后一轮演练的内容就是「人海战术」。
t_en: Correct. This final drill stage tests out your human-wave attack strategy.

t_ch: 没有捷径——只有战斗。
t_en: There's no shortcut — only combat.

t_ch: 下次吧。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 明白。一切服从你的安排，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: Got it. Everything's been done as per your order, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 关于之前的演练…
t_en: About the previous drills...

t_ch: 哦，我听说了！你的表现之精彩，让参加了演练的铁卫都心服口服。
t_en: Oh, I heard that! Your performance has been so wonderful that all the Silvermane Guards you faced off against have been won over.

t_ch: 你的名声已经传遍铁卫禁区了，{NICKNAME}。大家都盼着能和你再多交流交流呢。
t_en: Your reputation has spread like wildfire throughout the restricted zone, {NICKNAME}. We're all looking forward to engaging frequently with you.

t_ch: ——不过，我猜你们手头上还有更要紧的事，对吧？那就祝你们万事顺利。
t_en: ...But I guess you have more pressing matters to attend to, yes? Then I wish you all the best.

t_ch: 哔——啵。
t_en: Beep-boop.

t_ch: 打的漂亮，{NICKNAME}教官。真希望我也能跟你一样强。
t_en: Well done, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}. I hope I can one day be as strong as you.

t_ch: 我要去找少校制定训练计划了。我的下一个目标是当上队长。
t_en: I'm off to make a training plan with the major. My next goal is to make it to captain.

t_ch: 谢谢你给了我启发，{NICKNAME}教官。哔——啵。
t_en: Thank you for enlightening me, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}. Beep-boop.

t_ch: 还能再组织一次禁区演练吗？
t_en: Can we put together another round of restricted zone drills?

t_ch: 当然可以。不过军费有限，重复演练可就没有配发的奖励了。
t_en: Sure. However, we have a limited military budget, so no rewards will be given for repeating drills.

t_ch: 你确定要再参与一次演练吗，{NICKNAME}教官？
t_en: Are you sure you want to do another round of drills, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 开始吧。
t_en: Let's begin.

t_ch: 哔——啵。收到指示。立刻开始布置演练场地。
t_en: Beep-boop. Instructions received. Setting up the drill venue...

t_ch: 还是算了。
t_en: Forget it.

t_ch: 哔——啵。再见，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: Beep-boop. Goodbye, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 祝你好运。
t_en: Good luck.

t_ch: 哔——啵。再见，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: Beep-boop. Goodbye, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教官，又见面了！哈！哈！哈！
t_en: We meet again, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}! Bwahaha!

t_ch: 不得不说，能认识你是我莫大的荣幸。我要把见证你作战英姿的数据多备份几次！
t_en: I have to say, meeting you has been my greatest honor. I'd love to capture lots more data about your heroic combat moves!

t_ch: 那帮不敬业的工程师，稍微遇到点小故障就要把我的系统格式化——我才不会让他们得逞，哈！哈！哈！
t_en: Those cursed engineers are always formatting my hard disk whenever having the slightest glitch — but I won't let them get away with it. Bwahaha!

t_ch: 喔唷，{NICKNAME}！你居然真来了！
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! I can't believe you are here!

t_ch: 我本来都不指望了——毕竟你之前已经帮了我们那么多，再提要求就有点蛮不讲理的意思了。
t_en: I didn't expect you would come. After all, you have offered us a lot of help. I'll be imposing on you if I ask anything more from you.

t_ch: 我享受和裂界战斗。
t_en: I enjoy fighting the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 唷，血气方刚！有我年轻时的派头，哈哈哈！
t_en: Wow, I admire your fighting spirit! You remind me of myself when I was young, hahaha!

t_ch: 有奖励都好说。
t_en: As long as I'll be rewarded, I'm open to any missions.

t_ch: 那是当然，这怎么可能让你白出力呢？于情于理都说不过去啊。
t_en: Of course, you will. How can we make you work for nothing?

t_ch: 惩奸除恶，义不容辞。
t_en: I'll do my best to punish and eliminate the evil.

t_ch: 唷，大义凛然！有我年轻时的派头，哈哈哈！
t_en: Wow, you're so righteous! You remind me of myself when I was young, hahaha!

t_ch: 行。既然你都答应了，我也就不客气了。
t_en: Alright. You've agreed. Let's ramp things up.

t_ch: 这次行动的目标简单明了——扫荡「残响回廊」，把一路上撞见的裂界怪物全都清理干净。
t_en: This next goal is simple and clear — charge the Corridor of Fading Echoes and defeat all the Fragmentum monsters there.

t_ch: 哦，对了…技术部之前告诉我，他们捣鼓出了些新玩意，说不定能在扫荡行动里派上大用场。
t_en: Oh yeah... the Technology Division told me that they came up with a few new gadgets, which might just be useful for wiping out all those monsters.

t_ch: 我离开上层太久，早就看不懂那些稀奇古怪的发明了。不过谁知道呢？指不定有惊喜，哈哈。
t_en: I've been away from the Overworld so long now that I've long given up hope of understanding those weird gizmos. But, who knows? Maybe we'll be surprised, haha.

t_ch: 你可别放松警惕，这活绝对不轻松。我只是不习惯在出战前把气氛搞得太压抑罢了。
t_en: Whatever you do, don't let your guard down. This ain't gonna be easy. I'm not really the best at delivering serious pre-combat pep talks.

t_ch: 你再检查检查，等准备周全了就来找我吧。预祝咱们合作愉快，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Keep running checks, and come back to me when you're ready. I'm looking forward to our cooperation already, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 您、您好！您就是大名鼎鼎的{NICKNAME}，对吧？
t_en: H—Hello! Are you the {NICKNAME} I've heard so much about?

t_ch: 是我。
t_en: It's me.

t_ch: 能、能认识您十分荣幸！
t_en: I—It's an honor to meet you!

t_ch: 奥列格兵长已经入驻前线了…他说过您一定会来的！
t_en: Captain Oleg's already hit the front lines... He said that you'd definitely come!

t_ch: 奥列格人呢？
t_en: Where's Oleg?

t_ch: 奥、奥列格兵长已经入驻前线了…他说过您一定会来的！
t_en: C—Captain Oleg's already hit the front lines... He said that you'd definitely come!

t_ch: 能、能认识您，我倍感荣幸！
t_en: I—I'm honored to meet you!

t_ch: 我、我来给您复述一下这次的行动目标：呃…扫荡「残响回廊」，清理所有出没的裂界怪物。对，通报上是这么写的。
t_en: L—Let me recap your target this time: Err... Charge the Corridor of Fading Echoes, and defeat all the Fragmentum monsters lurking there. Yes, that's what it says here in the memo.

t_ch: 对、对了，听说技术部提前在前线布置好了先进的御敌装置，可以在作战中使用。好像是叫…叫…哦，叫<color=#dbc291ff>「照雪灯炉」</color>。
t_en: O—Oh yeah, the Technology Division told me that they'd set up advanced enemy defenses on the front line which could be used in combat. I th—think they're called... They're called <color=#dbc291ff>Snowshine Lamps</color>.

t_ch: 装置的具、具体使用方法，您可以在前线咨询奥列格兵长。
t_en: Ask Captain Oleg on the front lines about how to use it.

t_ch: 您、您如果准备好上前线了，就请跟我说一声。我、我带您去找奥列格兵长。
t_en: I—If you're ready to go to the front lines now, just let me know. I—I'll take you to see Captain Oleg.

t_ch: 出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 太、太好了！奥列格兵长说了，只要您出手，裂界怪物就只有逃、逃命的份儿……
t_en: G—Great! Captain Oleg said, if you're here, then the Fragmentum monsters will h—have to just flee in fear...

t_ch: 这、这边走！
t_en: Th—This way, please!

t_ch: 再给我讲讲这次的行动计划。
t_en: Tell me about the plan for this mission.

t_ch: 我、我来给您复述一下这次的行动目标：呃…扫荡「残响回廊」，清理所有出没的裂界怪物。对，是这么写的。
t_en: L—Let me recap your target this time: Err... Charge the Corridor of Fading Echoes, and defeat all the Fragmentum monsters lurking there. Yes, that's what it says here.

t_ch: 对、对了，听说技术部提前在前线布置好了先进的御敌装置，可以在作战中使用。好像是叫…叫…哦，叫<color=#dbc291ff>「照雪灯炉」</color>。
t_en: O—Oh yeah, the Technology Division told me that they'd set up advanced enemy defenses on the front line which could be used in combat. I th—think they're called... They're called <color=#dbc291ff>Snowshine Lamps</color>.

t_ch: 装置的具、具体使用方法，您可以在前线咨询奥列格兵长。
t_en: Ask Captain Oleg on the front lines about how to use it.

t_ch: 再等等。
t_en: One second.

t_ch: 没、没问题，您慢慢来。前路艰险，可得做好万全准备再出发。
t_en: N—No problem, take your time. It's dangerous ahead. Make sure you're completely prepared before you set out.

t_ch: 又、又见面了，贝洛伯格的英雄，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: {NICKNAME}! We m—meet again, Hero of Belobog.

t_ch: 奥列格兵长跟我复述了您作战的事迹。我、我…我非常崇拜您，希望有朝一日能和您并肩作战！
t_en: Captain Oleg has told me about your deeds in battle. I—I... I admire you so much, and hope to fight alongside you one day!

t_ch: 总有机会的。
t_en: I hope you get that chance.

t_ch: 我、我盼望那一天能早点到来！
t_en: I—I'm hoping that day can come soon!

t_ch: 你要努力活到那时候。
t_en: Keep on striving for it until your time comes.

t_ch: 我…我明白了，我绝对尽力而为！
t_en: I... I understand, and I'll do my utmost best!

t_ch: 那、那个，奥列格兵长请我向您转达一件事。
t_en: W—Well, Captain Oleg asked me to pass on a message to you.

t_ch: 裂界的怪物又涌进了残响回廊，兵长又、又上前线去勘察战况了。
t_en: The Fragmentum has spilled out into the Corridor of Fading Echoes, a—and he's gone to the front lines to survey the situation.

t_ch: 他还说，如果您还没有离、离开贝洛伯格的话，也许会想继续参与铁卫的作战行动……
t_en: He said that if you haven't l—left Belobog yet, you might be still keen to keep fighting in our sorties...

t_ch: 如、如果您有这个意思，我可以立刻带您上前线！
t_en: I—If you're keen, I can take you to the front lines immediately!

t_ch: 出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 收、收到，立刻出发！
t_en: R—Roger! Setting out!

t_ch: 再给我讲讲这次行动计划。
t_en: Tell me more about this current mission.

t_ch: 行动的目标和上次一样…扫、扫荡残响回廊，击败所有徘徊的敌人。
t_en: Our target is the same as last time: Ch—Charge the Corridor of Fading Echoes and defeat all the enemies lurking there.

t_ch: 再等等。
t_en: One second.

t_ch: 没、没问题，您慢慢来。前路艰险，可得做好万全准备再出发。
t_en: N—No problem, take your time. It's dangerous ahead. Make sure you're completely prepared before you set out.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！能见到您真是太好了。奥、奥列格兵长对您大加赞赏，他说您不止有过人的胆识，还有绝顶的头脑……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, it's so great to see you again! Captain O—Oleg is full of praise for you, saying that you're incredibly brave with brains to match...

t_ch: 他说的句句属实。
t_en: That's true.

t_ch: 大家都说谦虚是种美、美德，但如果有您的实力，应该也用不着谦虚了吧？
t_en: People say that modesty is a v—virtue, but if someone could be as powerful as you, why would they be humble about it?

t_ch: 他想捧杀我？
t_en: Is he baiting me into complacency?

t_ch: 奥列格兵长说这话的时候很真、真诚，我可以替他作证！
t_en: Captain Oleg was completely earnest wh—when he said that. I can vouch for that!

t_ch: 那个，既然您、您来了，我还是把最近的战况传、传达出来吧……
t_en: Then seeing as y—you're here, I may as well pass on the l—latest battle intel...

t_ch: 北边的状况不、不容乐观。裂界的反攻更加猛烈了，裂界怪、怪物的数量超出了铁卫的预期……
t_en: The situation in the northern front is not looking g—good. Counterattacks from the Fragmentum monsters are getting more violent, and their numbers far exceed our initial estimates...

t_ch: 虽然回廊里有奥列格兵长撑着，但我…我还是担心那里的局势。{NICKNAME}，你还会帮我们清扫战场吗？
t_en: Despite Captain Oleg being there, I... I'm still worried about our situation. {NICKNAME}, can you please help us clear the corridor?

t_ch: 别担心，我会出手。
t_en: Don't worry. I'll do it.

t_ch: 太、太好了！我这就带您去见奥列格兵长。希、希望这能是最后一次……
t_en: That's g—great! I'll take you to Captain Oleg now. I h—hope this is the last time...

t_ch: 再给我讲讲这次行动计划。
t_en: Tell me more about this current mission.

t_ch: 行动的目标和前两次一样…扫、扫荡残响回廊，击败所有徘徊的敌人。
t_en: Our target is the same as the last two times: Ch—Charge the Corridor of Fading Echoes and defeat all the enemies lurking there.

t_ch: 再等等。
t_en: One second.

t_ch: 没、没问题，您慢慢来。前路艰险，可得做好万全准备再出发。
t_en: N—No problem, take your time. It's dangerous ahead. Make sure you're completely prepared before you set out.

t_ch: 残响回廊后来怎么样了？
t_en: What happened at the Corridor of Fading Echoes?

t_ch: 噢，你还惦记着这事呢？真是个热心肠啊，哈哈哈。
t_en: Oh, you're still thinking about that? What a kindhearted person, hahaha.

t_ch: 实话说，没什么变化。我们每清理一群杂鱼，就会有更多的怪物从裂界里涌出来。
t_en: Honestly, nothing much has changed. Every time we clear out one swarm, more monsters just pour out of the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 但我们不会放弃。只要还有明天，我们就会坚持战斗下去。
t_en: But we cannot give up. As long as there's a tomorrow, we will continue with our fight.

t_ch: 「明天」——它是你帮我们争取来的宝贝，{NICKNAME}。我们不会挥霍它的。
t_en: "Tomorrow" — now that's a luxury that you've won for us, {NICKNAME}. We certainly won't squander it.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，又见面了！您好！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, we meet again! Hello there!

t_ch: 最近怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 还能说什么呢？简直精神焕发。
t_en: What can I say? Simply splendid.

t_ch: 而且，不知道您注意到了没——我说话不利索的毛病已经彻底改正了！
t_en: And I'm not sure if you've noticed — but all my stuttering has been completely fixed!

t_ch: 你口齿好像变清楚了。
t_en: Your articulation sounds a lot better.

t_ch: 您发现了？太好了！我正想告诉您，我说话不利索的毛病已经彻底改正了！
t_en: You can tell? That's great! I'm just about to tell you how my stuttering has been completely fixed!

t_ch: 这都多亏了您——听说了您在前线的英勇事迹之后，我觉得自己绝对不能再停滞不前…是时候做出改变了。
t_en: It's all thanks to you — after hearing about your heroic deeds on the front lines, I've resolved to get myself out of this rut... It's time for a change.

t_ch: 我虽然没法像您一样驰骋沙场，但还是可以从一些小事做起的。您看，我只是稍微逼了自己一把，就把口齿不清的毛病改掉了。
t_en: I can't rush headlong into the fray as you can, but I can start with the small things. Check it out. I've just gotten rid of my stutter by pushing myself a little.

t_ch: 我接下来的梦想就是追上您的步伐，有朝一日能和您并肩作战！
t_en: My next dream is to follow in your steps so that one day we can fight together alongside each other!

t_ch: 加油吧。
t_en: Keep up the good work.

t_ch: 你的梦想一定会实现的。
t_en: Your dreams will surely come true.

t_ch: 感谢您鼓励的话！不过话说回来……
t_en: Thanks for the words of encouragement! But then again...

t_ch: 北边前线传来消息，残响回廊里的怪物似乎又卷土重来了。
t_en: News from the northern front informs that the monsters at the Corridor of Fading Echoes are mounting a comeback.

t_ch: 那些家伙真是没完没了，怎么烧也烧不完。前线缺少了您的战斗力，只靠铁卫兄弟们也很难再进一步了。
t_en: Those guys just never give up, and we can't seem to deliver the final blow. Without your prowess on the front lines, it's hard to make any progress just by relying on the Silvermane Guards.

t_ch: 别担心，我会出手。
t_en: Don't worry. I'll do it.

t_ch: 真的吗？可是我们没有什么东西能作为回报…即使这样您也愿意出手相助吗？
t_en: Really? But we don't have anything to offer in return... Would you still be willing to help out, anyway?

t_ch: 不必多说，带路吧。
t_en: Say no more. Take me there now.

t_ch: …感激不尽！我们这就出发，目标：残响回廊！
t_en: ...I'm so grateful! Let's go now. Our target: The Corridor of Fading Echoes!

t_ch: 以后一定。
t_en: Next time for sure.

t_ch: 明白！您已经帮了我们太多——接下来的事就交给我们吧。
t_en: Got it! You've helped us out so much already, so leave the rest up to us.

t_ch: 希望我们很快还能再见，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Hope to see you soon, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 我会在身后祝福他们。
t_en: I will pray for their triumphant return.

t_ch: 谢谢！您已经帮了我们太多——接下来的事就交给我们吧。
t_en: Thanks! You've helped us out so much already, so leave the rest up to us.

t_ch: 希望我们很快还能再见，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Hope to see you soon, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 北边前线传来消息，残响回廊里的怪物似乎又卷土重来了。
t_en: News from the northern front informs that the monsters at the Corridor of Fading Echoes are mounting a comeback.

t_ch: 那些家伙真是没完没了，怎么烧也烧不完。前线缺少了您的战斗力，只靠铁卫兄弟们也很难再进一步了。
t_en: Those guys just never give up, and we can't seem to deliver the final blow. Without your prowess on the front lines, it's hard to make any progress just by relying on the Silvermane Guards.

t_en: Don't worry. I'll do it.

t_en: Really? But we don't have anything to offer in return... Would you still be willing to help out, anyway?

t_en: Say no more. Take me there now.

t_ch: …感激不尽！我们这就出发，目标：残响回廊！
t_en: ...I'm so grateful! Let's go now. Our target: The Corridor of Fading Echoes!

t_ch: 以后一定。
t_en: Next time for sure.

t_ch: 明白！您已经帮了我们太多——接下来的事就交给我们吧。
t_en: Got it! You've helped us out so much already, so leave the rest up to us.

t_ch: 希望我们很快还能再见，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Hope to see you soon, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 我会在身后祝福他们。
t_en: I will pray for their triumphant return.

t_ch: 谢谢！您已经帮了我们太多——接下来的事就交给我们吧。
t_en: Thanks! You've helped us out so much already, so leave the rest up to us.

t_ch: 希望我们很快还能再见，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Hope to see you soon, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 你好，开拓者。你要前往造物之柱吗？
t_en: Hello, Trailblazer. Do you want to go to the Pillars of Creation?

t_ch: 请带路吧。
t_en: Please lead the way.

t_ch: 跟我来吧——山路崎岖，还请小心脚下。
t_en: Come with me. The mountain roads are treacherous, please watch your step.

t_ch: 还是算了。
t_en: I'll pass.

t_ch: 一路小心，我会一直在这里执勤。
t_en: Take care, I shall be here on duty whenever you need.

t_ch: 你好，我是星际和平公司的员工，负责在此接引来客。你打算去旧武器试验场吗？
t_en: Hello, I am an IPC employee, I am in charge of welcoming travelers. Are you planning a visit to the Old Weapon Testing Ground?

t_ch: 带我去吧。
t_en: Lead the way.

t_ch: 好的，希望你能在那里感受到公司的诚意。
t_en: Very well, I hope that your visit shows you the sincere intentions of the IPC.

t_ch: 之后再说。
t_en: Maybe later.

t_ch: 好的，随时欢迎你参观那里的工程。
t_en: Very well, you are always welcome to visit and view the construction project.

t_ch: 尊敬的{NICKNAME}，佩佩已抵达目标舱段。
t_en: Dear {NICKNAME}, Peppy has arrived at the designated zone.

t_ch: 舱段已封锁，是否进入。
t_en: The zone has been sealed off. Do we enter?

t_ch: 现在出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 我还没准备好。
t_en: I'm not ready yet.

t_ch: （那是？佩佩？）
t_en: (Is that... Peppy?)

t_ch: 汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof!

t_ch: 拯救佩佩。
t_en: Rescue Peppy.

t_ch: 汪汪？汪汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof? Woof woof woof!

t_ch: 佩佩示意你触摸它背包的某处，你按下按钮——
t_en: Peppy indicates for you to touch somewhere on its backpack. You press a button...

t_ch: 你听见细微的电子音：「联觉信标已激活。」
t_en: You hear a faint electronic voice: The Synesthesia Beacon has been activated.

t_ch: （汪，听到了吗？汪？）
t_en: (Woof, can you hear me? Woof?)

t_ch: 是狗在说话！
t_en: You're... talking!

t_ch: （这不是很正常吗？）
t_en: (Is that not normal?)

t_ch: 我幻听了？
t_en: Am I hearing things?

t_ch: （太笨啦，是佩佩在跟你说话！）
t_en: (Stop being stupid. Peppy is talking to you!)

t_ch: 在激活信标后，你意识到，这或许就是艾丝妲所说的「惊喜」。
t_en: After activating the beacon, you realize that this could be the "surprise" that Asta mentioned.

t_ch: （别发呆了！）
t_en: (Stop gawking!)

t_ch: 所以你在这儿干嘛？
t_en: So what are you doing here?

t_ch: （勇敢佩佩，努力探案！）
t_en: (Brave Peppy is hot on the trail!)

t_ch: 艾丝妲很担心你…
t_en: Asta is really worried about you...

t_ch: （佩佩知道，所以佩佩要努力探案，为小姐减轻负担！）
t_en: (Peppy knows, and that's why Peppy is investigating hard, to reduce the burden on Miss Asta!)

t_ch: （信号有点问题，我没法联系上小姐。）
t_en: (There's some problem with the signal — I can't contact Miss Asta.)

t_ch: （我闻到了陌生的味道，还夹杂着「分装快乐」的气息，我怕打草惊蛇，才守在这里。）
t_en: (I smelled something strange that had the lingering whiff of the Joy Filtration Set. I didn't want to tip anybody off, so I decided to stay guard here.)

t_ch: （我给你带路，走吧！）
t_en: (I'll lead the way. Let's go!)

t_ch: 汪汪…汪！汪！
t_en: Woof woof... Woof! Woof!

t_ch: （门卡住了…整个区域的门禁系统都坏了汪？）
t_en: (The door is stuck... The entire area's access control system is down, woof?)

t_ch: 后退，我来砸门。
t_en: Back up, I'll smash the door in.

t_ch: 你拿出棒球棍，冲着金属大门哐哐猛砸，震得你虎口发麻，大门却不为所动。
t_en: You whip out a baseball bat and start flailing at the metal door with reckless abandon. The rebounding reverberations cause your whole body to go numb, but the door does not budge.

t_ch: 进不去啊，怎么办？
t_en: We can't get in. What now?

t_ch: 佩佩歪着头沉思了起来，你努力克制着抚摸它毛茸茸的头顶的欲望。
t_en: Peppy tilts its head in contemplation. You resist the desire to ruffle its fluffy head.

t_ch: （这个高度…好像可以钻过去汪？）
t_en: (This height... I think we can get through, woof?)

t_ch: （但{NICKNAME}长这么高，蹲下来会被卡住吧？还是得靠佩佩我啊！）
t_en: (But {NICKNAME} is so tall. Won't you get stuck if you crouch? Better leave it to Peppy!)

t_ch: （{NICKNAME}，现在，伸手摸我——不是让你摸我的头，你找下我背上的装置，看到那个按钮了嘛？）
t_en: (Now, {NICKNAME}, reach out and touch me. No, not my head! Look for a button on my back — do you see it?)

t_ch: 在佩佩的指导下，你找到了它所说的按钮并按了下去。
t_en: With Peppy's instructions, you find the button it is talking about and press down on it.

t_ch: 你听到了一阵冰冷的电子音：「是否开启同步模式？」
t_en: You hear an emotionless electronic voice: "Activate sync mode?"

t_ch: （同步模式就是让你连接我的思维信号，我们通过信标交流，快试试。）
t_en: (You can connect to my thought signal with sync mode. We can communicate via the beacon. Give it a try.)

t_ch: 开启同步模式。
t_en: Activate sync mode.

t_ch: 一种强烈的眩晕感朝你袭来，你扶额，低头望向自己的鞋尖——
t_en: An intense wave of dizziness hits you. You clutch your temple, lowering your head, and stare at the toes of your shoes...

t_ch: 你感到异样，那毛茸茸的一团，似乎是你的…脚？
t_en: You feel strange. That fluffy pile, it appears to be your... feet?

t_ch: 救命，我变成狗了！
t_en: Help, I've turned into a dog!

t_ch: （狗狗有什么不好吗？）
t_en: (What's wrong with being a dog?)

t_ch: 我这是在做梦吗？
t_en: Am I dreaming?

t_ch: （梦里会有佩佩吗？）
t_en: (Do you think I'd be in your dream?)

t_ch: （这是小姐特意给我定制的信标功能，很神奇吧！）
t_en: (This is the custom beacon that Missy gave me. It's awesome!)

t_ch: （还要去追踪失窃的奇物呢，走吧。）
t_en: (We still have to track down the missing curio. C'mon.)

t_ch: （快住手，别砸了。这门很重的，要是砸坏了，突然掉下来会砸伤你的。还是想想别的办法吧？）
t_en: (Stop smashing it! This door is super heavy. If you smash it open, you'll be in serious trouble when it falls on you. Let's think of another way.)

t_ch: 一种强烈的眩晕感朝你袭来，你扶额，低头望向自己的鞋尖——
t_en: An intense wave of dizziness hits you. You clutch your temple, lowering your head, and stare at the toes of your shoes...

t_ch: 你感到异样，那毛茸茸的一团，似乎是你的…脚？
t_en: You feel strange. That fluffy pile, it appears to be your... feet?

t_ch: 救命，我变成狗了！
t_en: Help, I've turned into a dog!

t_ch: （狗狗有什么不好吗？）
t_en: (What's wrong with being a dog?)

t_ch: 我这是在做梦吗？
t_en: Am I dreaming?

t_ch: （梦里会有佩佩吗？）
t_en: (Do you think I'd be in your dream?)

t_ch: （这是小姐特意给我定制的信标功能，很神奇吧！）
t_en: (This is the custom beacon that Missy gave me. It's awesome!)

t_ch: （还要去追踪失窃的奇物呢，走吧。）
t_en: (We still have to track down the missing curio. C'mon.)

t_ch: （这些千纸鹤是奇物的动态密保，它们会根据奇物的定位实时改变位置……）
t_en: (These origami cranes serve as the dynamic security for the Curio. They will change their location in real-time based on the Curio's position...)

t_ch: （跟着这些千纸鹤走，「分装快乐」肯定就在前面！）
t_en: (Follow these origami cranes. The Joy Filtration Set is definitely up ahead!)

t_ch: （糟了…这些大家伙，我可打不过它们……）
t_en: (Oh no... I can't beat these giant baddies...)

t_ch: （对了，我体积小，或许能利用附近的物件挡住它们的视线。）
t_en: (Oh yeah, I'm small. I can use nearby objects to obstruct their line of sight.)

t_ch: （{NICKNAME}，接下来由你来指挥，带我从这里穿过去——一定要保证我不被怪物发现噢。）
t_en: ({NICKNAME}, you'll be in command to lead me across. You must promise me that we won't be spotted by the monsters.)

t_ch: （被发现了！快跑！）
t_en: (We've been made! Run!)

t_ch: （还好我身上有喷气式推进装置，这些怪物没想到吧，佩佩还能飞！）
t_en: (Good thing I've got a jet booster equipped. I bet these monsters didn't think I could fly!)

t_ch: （这是最近的迫降点了，{NICKNAME}，等会儿可千万要小心些，别再被它们逮到了。）
t_en: (This is the nearest landing point. {NICKNAME}, you must be super careful later. Don't get caught by them again.)

t_ch: （救救…救救……）
t_en: (Help... Help...)

t_ch: （我好像听到了什么东西在叫，像是呼救 ？）
t_en: (I thought I heard something, like someone calling out for help?)

t_ch: （看来还有其他人被困在这里，我们要不要过去看看？）
t_en: (Looks like there's someone else stuck here. Should we go take a look?)

t_ch: （陌生的气息就在这里…{NICKNAME}，过去看看吧？）
t_en: (There's a strange air about here... {NICKNAME}, come take a look?)

t_ch: （刚刚是你在求救吗？）
t_en: (Were you calling for help just now?)

t_ch: 迷路了…谢谢…谢谢你……
t_en: I got lost... Thank you... Thank you...

t_ch: 嘤…谢谢…谢谢你……
t_en: Eeep... Thank... Thank you...
Eeep...

t_ch: 就是你偷了奇物？
t_en: You were the one who stole the Curio?

t_ch: 什么是…奇物……
t_en: What is a... Curio...

t_ch: 人赃并获！虽然你不是人…
t_en: Caught you red-handed! You're not human though...

t_ch: 我不是人…你也不是……
t_en: I'm not human... and neither are you...

t_ch: 感觉没吃饱……
t_en: I think I'm still hungry...

t_ch: （「分装快乐」表面有打开的痕迹，该不会……）
t_en: (There are signs that the Joy Filtration Set has been opened. It couldn't be...)

t_ch: 你究竟对奇物做了什么？
t_en: What exactly have you done with the Curio?

t_ch: 吃了…甜甜的……
t_en: I ate... something sweet...

t_ch: （这家伙可真生猛，没想到它把「分装快乐」当成宠物罐头吃了。）
t_en: (Gosh, that fellow's feisty. It ate the Joy Filtration Set like it was canned pet food.)

t_ch: （没办法，只能这么回报给小姐再处理了。）
t_en: (There's no other way. I'll have to report this to Missy first.)

t_ch: （好了，没你什么事了，这里不太安全，你还是别待着这儿。）
t_en: (All right, you're in the clear now. It's not safe here, so you'd better not linger.)

t_ch: 嘤嘤……
t_en: Eeep...

t_ch: 你们…别走…我有个朋友被困在这里了嘤…它不敢出来…你们能帮帮忙吗？
t_en: Don't... Don't go... A friend of mine is stuck inside here... It dares not come out... Eeep, can you help?

t_ch: （{NICKNAME}，要过去看看吗？）
t_en: ({NICKNAME}, wanna have a look?)

t_ch: 是不是刚才遇到的那个？
t_en: Is it the one we just met?

t_ch: 嘤…你身上好像有它的味道…还真是，谢谢嘤……
t_en: Eeep... you do have its smell on you... You're right, thank you...

t_ch: 可以啊，那就去找呗。
t_en: Sure, let's go look for it.

t_ch: 好嘤！你们人好好哦！
t_en: Eeep! You are kind people!

t_ch: 还是先回去复命吧。
t_en: Let's go back and report in first.

t_ch: 好吧…不打扰你们了嘤……
t_en: Okay... I'll stop bothering you eeep...

t_ch: 汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof!

t_ch: 喊我来干嘛？
t_en: Why'd you yell for me?

t_ch: 小狗用它毛茸茸的头拱了拱你的手，你熟练地按下了它背上的信标按钮。
t_en: The puppy nudges your hand with its fluffed head. You instinctively press the beacon button on its back.

t_ch: （原来丢失的「分装快乐」不止一套，另外一套的下落嘛…我找到线索了。）
t_en: (There is more than one Joy Filtration Set lost. As for the other set's whereabouts... I've found a clue.)

t_ch: （佩佩和{NICKNAME}是天下第一好的搭档，你会帮佩佩的对吧？）
t_en: (Peppy and {NICKNAME} are the world's best detective duo! You'll help Peppy, right?)

t_ch: 小狗湿漉漉的舌头舔了舔你的手，见你不为所动，又咬着你的手套不肯松口。
t_en: The puppy's sloppy tongue licks your hand, but seeing that you are unmoved, bites down on your glove and refuses to let go.

t_ch: 真是拿你没办法。
t_en: Fine, I'll play along.

t_ch: 佩佩朝你露出了个灿烂的笑容。
t_en: Peppy flashes a splendid smile at you.

t_ch: 当然，我们天下第一好！
t_en: Of course, we're the world's best!
